content.rendered:
Bruce Springsteen and Zach Bryan have a bit of a bromance going. We can feel their mutual appreciation and admiration in their 2024 Rolling Stone interview.

That interview was filmed shortly after Springsteen joined Bryan on stage in Brooklyn for a song they’d soon record together (“Sandpaper”) and another that Bruce had nothing to do with.

“Revival” was the closing track of Bryan’s second album, and even if they hadn’t yet met at the time Zach wrote it, t’s a track that invites comparison to Bruce’s own music with themes of communal catharsis, working-class heroes and the power of a party.

Bruce went on to share the stage with Zach twice more since that first night at Barclays–both times in stadiums, a testament to Bryan’s remarkable career trajectory.

On both occasions, they performed “Atlantic City” with Bruce very much in the spotlight, but in the show-closing “Revival”… well, that song is always about the party.

Revival
First performed: March 27, 2024 (Brooklyn, NY)
Last performed: July 20, 2025 (East Rutherford, NJ)

© July 11, 2026

jetpack-related-posts.title: Hey Tonight
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only (in public at least): Bruce Springsteen covers John Fogerty's 1971 hit single with John Eddie, Greg Kihn, Elliott Murphy, and Marshall Crenshaw.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Born on the Bayou
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce Springsteen and John Fogerty trade lead vocals on a smoking performance of "Born on the Bayou" at the 1993 Rock and Roll Hall of Fame induction.

jetpack-related-posts.title: I Fought the Law
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: In their first and only on-stage team-up, Don Henley and Bruce Springsteen covered an early rock classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 10
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Not one but *two* Springsteen box sets were released on this date: Live 1975-85 and Tracks. Plus: a Billy Joel guest appearance, a Daily Show interview, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 27
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Steel Mill opens for Black Sabbath, Bruce makes a Tonight Show appearance, and it's the world premiere of "It's the Little Things That Count." More highlights inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Odds of the Blues
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Like Bruce Springsteen, Texas songwriter Joe Ely crafts brawny, epic song-stories about good-hearted crooks, blue-collar heroes and enduring lovers. In fact, the primary difference between Springsteen and Ely comes down to accent'"  --Chicago Tribune, November 30, 2001 We lost the late, great Joe Ely last year, not long after Bruce…
